meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.

globalAlerts.warning: HARD MANDATE (NO EXCEPTIONS): For EVERY bug/regression in scratch/current-state.md, you MUST (1) replicate with HIGH CONFIDENCE via a failing test, (2) validate that replication FAILS via make target, and ONLY THEN (3) apply a fix. No exceptions.
ANTI-HALLUCINATION CONSTRAINT: Do NOT invent root causes or describe nonexistent logic. If you have not read the relevant code paths, add an investigation task.
TOOLING REQUIREMENT: All verification MUST use the make-based workflow. Cross-platform checks MUST pass (make all). macOS-only integration tests via project.mk targets.
RULE OF TWO (COMMIT GATE): No commits until two contiguous, issue-free subagent strict reviews confirm correctness.
INDEFINITE CYCLE: The task list must never become empty. Always expand scope towards True Perfection.

RESUME EXISTS: --resume flag, loadPlan() and savePlan() are ALREADY IMPLEMENTED in pr_split_script.js. Resume tasks are about IMPROVING UX, not implementing from scratch. But verify it actually works before assuming.

osm:pty DISTINCTION: The JavaScript module 'osm:pty' (registered in internal/builtin/register.go, defined in internal/builtin/pty/module.go) is UNUSED dead code — safe to remove. The underlying Go package internal/builtin/pty/ is CRITICAL — used by claudemux for PTY spawning. DO NOT delete the Go package.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Execute tasks strictly in sequence. Update blueprint.json incrementally after each task.
mandatoryDirectives.sourceOfTruth: Primary: scratch/current-state.md. Code: internal/command/pr_split_script.js (sendToHandle function search 'function sendToHandle', executeSplit search 'function executeSplit', mcpConfigPath search 'mcpConfigPath is required', ClaudeCodeExecutor search 'function ClaudeCodeExecutor'). Go: internal/command/pr_split.go (prSplitSendWithCancel search 'func prSplitSendWithCancel'). PTY: internal/builtin/pty/pty_unix.go (Spawn search 'func Spawn', Signal search 'func (h *unixProcessHandle) Signal'). TUI: internal/termmux/ui/autosplit.go (AutoSplitModel, renderSeparator search 'func (m AutoSplitModel) renderSeparator'). Tests: internal/command/pr_split_integration_test.go, main_test.go.
mandatoryDirectives.fullEndToEndIntegrationTesting: The -integration flag, -claude-command, -claude-arg flags registered in internal/command/main_test.go TestMain. project.mk targets: integration-test-prsplit, integration-test-prsplit-mcp, integration-test-termmux. New test functions in internal/command/ matching TestIntegration_* are automatically picked up by make integration-test-prsplit-mcp.

Windows verification skipped. Inserting T04a: Add delay. T04b: Create real E2E test using -claude-command. T04c: Run make-all-run-windows.

replanLog.trigger: Session: Test file split + async infrastructure overhaul
replanLog.summary: pr_split_test.go split into 12 new satellite files. dispatchAwaitPromise, evalJS, evalJSAsync rewritten for proper Promise-aware dispatch. Handler type assertion fallback added. All ~97 failures resolved → make all green.

sequentialTasks.task: TSplit-1: Split pr_split_test.go into manageable files
sequentialTasks.description: Split the 10,645-line pr_split_test.go into ~650 lines infrastructure-only + 12 new satellite test files: pr_split_cmd_meta_test.go, pr_split_heuristic_run_test.go, pr_split_claude_config_test.go, pr_split_template_unit_test.go, pr_split_mode_autofix_test.go, pr_split_conflict_retry_test.go, pr_split_local_integration_test.go, pr_split_scope_misc_test.go, pr_split_tui_subcommands_test.go, pr_split_session_cancel_test.go, pr_split_edge_hardening_test.go, pr_split_autosplit_recovery_test.go.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: pr_split_test.go contains only infrastructure (git helpers, TestPipeline, dispatch/eval functions). All test functions moved to appropriate satellite files. make all passes.

sequentialTasks.task: TSplit-2: Async dispatch + evalJS infrastructure for Promise-aware testing
sequentialTasks.description: Rewrote dispatchAwaitPromise (duck-typed .then/.catch Promise chaining with handler type fallback via goja.AssertFunction). Rewrote evalJS with two-path approach: await-containing JS → async IIFE wrapper, statement/expression JS without await → direct vm.RunString with Promise detection. Fixed evalJSAsync for consistency. Fixed TestPrSplitCommand_ClaudeFixFixWithoutExecutor to await async claude-fix strategy. Files: pr_split_test.go (infrastructure), pr_split_mode_autofix_test.go (await fix).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All ~200 pr-split tests pass. dispatchAwaitPromise properly awaits async command handlers. evalJS handles statements, expressions, and await expressions. make make-all-with-log exit 0.

sequentialTasks.task: T01: Run make all to establish clean baseline

sequentialTasks.acceptance: make all passes with zero failures.

sequentialTasks.task: T02: Run integration-test-prsplit-mcp to validate existing mock infrastructure
sequentialTasks.description: Run `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp` which executes TestIntegration_AutoSplitMockMCP. Requires no real AI. Validates mock MCP test infrastructure works.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: integration-test-prsplit-mcp passes or produces an understood skip.

sequentialTasks.task: T03: Regression replication — sendToHandle single-write delivers paste, not submit
sequentialTasks.description: BEFORE fixing sendToHandle, write a test in internal/command/pr_split_integration_test.go that demonstrates the current behavior: sendToHandle sends text+newline as ONE write. This test should PASS with the current code (proving the regression exists in the code). Name it TestIntegration_SendToHandle_SingleWriteBaseline. The test should use the existing mock handle infrastructure (search for TestIntegration_SendToHandle_FallbackDirect pattern) and assert exactly 1 send call containing both text and newline concatenated. This test captures the known-broken behavior as a baseline.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test passes with current code, proving sendToHandle concatenates text+newline into a single write — the documented regression behavior.

sequentialTasks.task: T04: Fix sendToHandle to send text and Enter as separate writes
sequentialTasks.description:
In internal/command/pr_split_script.js, function sendToHandle (search 'function sendToHandle'): (1) Remove `var payload = text + '
'` pattern. (2) Send `text` first, then send `'
'` as a separate write. (3) For TUI path (autoSplitTUI.sendWithCancel): call twice — first with text, then with newline. (4) For fallback path (handle.send): call send(text), then send('
'). (5) ERROR HANDLING: If first write succeeds but second fails, treat as fatal pipeline error — log both, propagate single unified error. Do not retry just the newline. (6) Update misleading comment that claims 'single-write pattern is deterministic' to describe the two-write approach and WHY it's necessary (non-blocking TUI readers treat single-write as paste). EXISTING TESTS WILL BREAK: TestIntegration_SendToHandle_FallbackDirect (asserts 1 send), TestIntegration_SendToHandle_TUIPath (asserts 1 sendWithCancel call). These must be updated to assert 2 calls in the same commit.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: sendToHandle sends text and newline as two distinct writes. Both TUI and fallback paths updated. Comment corrected. Existing tests updated from asserting 1 write to asserting 2 writes.
sequentialTasks.status: Incomplete - PTY coalesces writes, need delay

sequentialTasks.task: T04a: REARCHITECT sendToHandle as async Promise-based function
sequentialTasks.description: The blocking time.Sleep approach (even in a spawned goroutine with select) VIOLATES the event loop contract. Go function calls from JS block the event loop until they return. CORRECT ARCHITECTURE: (1) Remove sendTextWithEnter Go function entirely. (2) In JS, make sendToHandle return a Promise. (3) Use setTimeout for the 10ms delay: return new Promise(resolve => { sendWithCancel(text); setTimeout(() => { sendWithCancel(newline); resolve({error:null}); }, 10); }). (4) Update ALL callers of sendToHandle to use await or .then() pattern. (5) This requires converting affected functions to async. Files: pr_split_script.js (sendToHandle, sendClassificationPrompt, etc.), pr_split.go (remove sendTextWithEnter binding).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: sendToHandle returns Promise. Delay uses JS setTimeout (not Go time.Sleep). Event loop never blocked. All callers use await. Tests verify async behavior.

sequentialTasks.task: T04b: Create REAL end-to-end integration test using TestMain -claude-command
sequentialTasks.description: Create TestIntegration_AutoSplit_RealE2E that: (1) Requires -integration and -claude-command flags. (2) Uses the TestMain flags -claude-command=ollama -claude-arg=launch -claude-arg=claude -claude-arg=--model=minimax-m2.5:cloud -claude-arg=-- (or similar). (3) Sets up a real git repo with feature branch. (4) Runs automatedSplit() with the REAL Claude binary. (5) Verifies prompt is SUBMITTED (not pasted) by checking Claude responds coherently. (6) This test MUST use skipIfNoClaude(t) and have explicit timeout. Run via make integration-test-prsplit.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Real Claude integration test exists that exercises actual prompt submission. Test passes with configured Claude command. Test skips gracefully when Claude not available.

sequentialTasks.acceptance: Windows tests pass OR documented gap with cross-compile verification.

sequentialTasks.acceptance: All tests pass. No regressions.

sequentialTasks.task: T06: Regression replication — executeSplit worktree checkout conflict
sequentialTasks.description: BEFORE fixing executeSplit, write a test that sets up a git repo with: (1) main worktree on a feature branch, (2) additional worktree with 'main' checked out. Create a minimal split plan and call the executeSplit JS function. The test should FAIL with 'already used by worktree' error, proving the regression exists. Name it TestIntegration_ExecuteSplit_WorktreeConflict. Use t.TempDir() for all directories.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test reliably reproduces the worktree checkout error from scratch/current-state.md regression #4.

sequentialTasks.task: T07: Fix executeSplit to create branches from base commit SHA
sequentialTasks.description: In internal/command/pr_split_script.js function executeSplit (search 'function executeSplit'): (1) Before the split loop, resolve baseBranch to a commit SHA: `var baseCommit = gitExec(dir, ['rev-parse', currentBase]).stdout.trim()`. (2) Replace `git checkout currentBase` with `git checkout -b <splitBranchName> <baseCommit>` to create split branch directly from the commit without checking out the base branch name. This avoids the worktree conflict entirely because we never try to checkout the base branch by name. (3) Ensure restoreBranch logic still works — at the end, restore to the branch we were on before, not the base.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: executeSplit succeeds when baseBranch is in another worktree. TestIntegration_ExecuteSplit_WorktreeConflict now passes.

sequentialTasks.task: T08: Run make all after executeSplit fix
sequentialTasks.description: Run `make make-all-with-log` and `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp`.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All tests pass. Worktree test passes.

sequentialTasks.task: T09: Regression replication — force-kill leaves orphaned descendants
sequentialTasks.description: BEFORE fixing PTY process groups, write a test that: (1) Spawns a PTY process (via internal/builtin/pty Spawn) that in turn creates a child subprocess (e.g., shell running 'sleep 3600' or a test helper that holds a lock file). (2) Sends SIGKILL via Process.Signal('SIGKILL'). (3) Asserts the child subprocess PID STILL EXISTS after the parent dies — proving orphan survival. Use deterministic PID checking (syscall.Kill(pid, 0)) with cleanup to prevent test pollution. Name it TestPTYSpawn_ForceKill_OrphanSurvival.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test demonstrates child process survives parent SIGKILL — proving the force-kill orphan bug exists in current code.

sequentialTasks.task: T10: Fix PTY spawn to use process groups (Setpgid)
sequentialTasks.description: In internal/builtin/pty/pty_unix.go, function Spawn (search 'func Spawn'): (1) Before cmd.Start(), set `cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}`. This creates a new process group for the child. (2) In unixProcessHandle.Signal() (search 'func (h *unixProcessHandle) Signal'), change from `h.cmd.Process.Signal(sig)` (which sends to PID) to `syscall.Kill(-h.cmd.Process.Pid, sig.(syscall.Signal))` (which sends to process GROUP via negative PID). (3) Handle the type assertion for non-syscall.Signal types gracefully. (4) This change is Unix-only (pty_unix.go), so Windows builds are unaffected.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: SIGKILL kills entire process group. TestPTYSpawn_ForceKill_OrphanSurvival now passes (orphan no longer survives). Clean SIGTERM shutdown still works. If sig type assertion to syscall.Signal fails, fall back to os.Process.Signal() for the individual PID — log a warning but do not panic.

sequentialTasks.task: T11: Run make all + Linux container after force-kill fix
sequentialTasks.description: Run `make make-all-with-log`, `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp`, and `make make-all-in-container` (Linux). The Setpgid change is Unix-only so Linux must also pass.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: macOS and Linux tests pass. Force-kill test demonstrates process group cleanup.

sequentialTasks.task: T12: Remove claude REPL command and add test proving removal
sequentialTasks.description: In internal/command/pr_split_script.js, the `claude` command is registered in the REPL (search for the claude command handler near line ~5685-5747). It calls claudeExecutor.spawn() without mcpConfigPath, which was the trigger for scratch/current-state.md regression #1. ROOT CAUSE: The REPL 'claude' command was never wired to receive mcpConfigPath from osm:mcpcallback (unlike auto-split which correctly provides it). Remove the entire claude command handler. Also add a test (TestIntegration_REPL_NoClaudeCommand) that starts a pr-split REPL session and verifies 'claude' is not a recognized command.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: 'claude spawn' no longer triggers mcpConfigPath error — the command doesn't exist. Test mechanically verifies removal.

sequentialTasks.task: T13: Remove claude-status REPL command and add test proving removal
sequentialTasks.description: In internal/command/pr_split_script.js, remove the `claude-status` command handler (search for 'claude-status' in the command registry). JUSTIFICATION: claude-status is the companion command to the claude command removed in T12. It checks status of a Claude session that can only be created via the (now-removed) claude command, making it dead code. scratch/current-state.md section 'Clarification' calls out that 'There doesn't appear to be much point in exposing the claude command to auto-split—remove it entirely? Same for claude-status.' Add a test (TestIntegration_REPL_NoClaudeStatusCommand) verifying removal.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: 'claude-status' is not available in REPL. Test mechanically verifies.

sequentialTasks.task: T15: Implement bell detection in termmux VTerm output
sequentialTasks.description: In internal/termmux/vt/ (the VTerm package), add detection of \x07 (BEL) bytes in the terminal output stream. When BEL is encountered during output processing, emit a structured notification. This should be a general-purpose building block — not Claude-specific. Investigate the VTerm write path (search for the Write or ProcessOutput method in internal/termmux/vt/) and add BEL detection there. Emit a callback or event that the termmux layer can consume.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: When a child PTY emits \x07, the VTerm layer detects it and provides a notification mechanism.

sequentialTasks.task: T16: Implement bell propagation to outer terminal from background panes
sequentialTasks.description: In the termmux mux layer (internal/termmux/termmux.go), when bell is detected in a background (non-focused) pane, write \x07 to stdout to propagate to the user's terminal. When the pane IS focused, bell passes through naturally in the output stream.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Background pane bell reaches user's terminal. Can be verified by checking for \x07 in captured test output.

sequentialTasks.task: T17: Implement persistent attention indicator in AutoSplitModel
sequentialTasks.description: In internal/termmux/ui/autosplit.go AutoSplitModel: (1) Add a 'needsAttention' bool field per pane. (2) Set it to true when bell fires from a background pane. (3) In renderSeparator (search 'func (m AutoSplitModel) renderSeparator'), add a persistent visual indicator when needsAttention is true (e.g., '🔔 Claude needs attention'). (4) Clear needsAttention when user switches to that pane (via Ctrl+] toggle). Use a distinct styling that is impossible to miss.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Persistent indicator appears on bell from background pane, remains until user switches to that pane, visually distinct.

sequentialTasks.task: T18: Add tests for bell propagation and attention indicator lifecycle
sequentialTasks.description: Tests in appropriate test files: (1) Bell in background pane → \x07 at outer terminal. (2) Attention indicator appears in rendered View() output when needsAttention is true. (3) Indicator clears on pane focus.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Tests prove bell/attention lifecycle works correctly.

sequentialTasks.task: T32: Make evalJS/evalJSAsync timeout configurable
sequentialTasks.description: Both evalJS (line 538) and evalJSAsync (line 637) in pr_split_test.go use hardcoded time.After(60s). The real Claude test passes timeoutMs to JS but evalJS kills at 60s before JS timeout fires. Add optional timeout parameter to loadPrSplitEngineWithEval closures; real Claude tests use 10min.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestIntegration_AutoSplitWithClaude_Pipeline uses 10min evalJS timeout. Unit tests still use 60s default. make test passes.
sequentialTasks.notes: _evalTimeout key extracted from overrides in loadPrSplitEngineWithEval. Both evalJS and evalJSAsync use configurable timeout. Real Claude tests set 10min. Unit tests default 60s. make all PASS.

sequentialTasks.task: T33: Convert sendWithRetry EAGAIN delay from blocking to async
sequentialTasks.description: In sendToHandle (pr_split_script.js), sendWithRetry uses timemod.sleep(10ms) — blocking Go call inside async JS, violating T04a principle. Convert sendWithRetry to async function using await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, EAGAIN_RETRY_DELAY_MS)).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: No timemod.sleep in sendToHandle. EAGAIN tests in pr_split_conflict_retry_test.go pass. make test passes.
sequentialTasks.notes: sendWithRetry converted to async. timemod import removed (dead code). Callers use await. All EAGAIN tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: T34: Test handle dying between text write and newline write
sequentialTasks.description: 10ms setTimeout gap between text and newline writes creates race window. If Claude process dies during delay, newline write hits dead handle. No existing test covers this. Add TestPrSplitCommand_SendToHandle_HandleDiesBetweenWrites in pr_split_conflict_retry_test.go.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test proves error is reported correctly when handle dies after text but before newline. make test passes.
sequentialTasks.notes: TestPrSplitCommand_SendToHandle_HandleDiesBetweenWrites added. Verifies broken pipe error on newline write with 2 send calls.

sequentialTasks.task: T35: Add EAGAIN retry test for the newline write
sequentialTasks.description: Existing EAGAIN test only covers text write. EAGAIN can also hit newline write independently. Add TestPrSplitCommand_SendToHandle_NewlineEAGAINRetry and NewlineEAGAINExhausted in pr_split_conflict_retry_test.go.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Tests cover EAGAIN on second write (newline). Both retry-success and retry-exhaustion paths tested. make test passes.
sequentialTasks.notes: NewlineEAGAINRetry (3 calls: text+EAGAIN+success) and NewlineEAGAINExhausted (5 calls: 1 text + 4 EAGAIN) both added and passing.

sequentialTasks.task: T36: Document dual-layer timeout architecture
sequentialTasks.description: Add 'Timeout Architecture' section to docs/pr-split-testing.md explaining three timeout layers: go test -timeout, Go evalJS time.After(60s), JS AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS.*TimeoutMs. Explain which killed the T27 real Claude test and how to configure for slow models.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Developer can configure timeouts for slow AI models from docs alone. make test passes.
sequentialTasks.notes: Timeout Architecture section added to docs/pr-split-testing.md with ASCII diagram, layer descriptions, interaction analysis, and EAGAIN retry docs.

sequentialTasks.task: T37: REAL Claude integration test — actually confirm it PASSES end-to-end
sequentialTasks.description: HANA MANDATE: Run make run-real-claude-test with the T32 timeout fix (10min evalJS timeout). The test MUST pass end-to-end: classification prompt sent, Claude responds with MCP tool call, plan generated, splits executed, equivalence verified. If it fails, diagnose and FIX the root cause — do NOT dismiss as 'config issue'. This is the HIGHEST priority task.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestIntegration_AutoSplitWithClaude_Pipeline passes with real Claude. Full 8-step pipeline completes. Test output captured and reviewed.
sequentialTasks.notes: Blocked: Claude Code not logged in (no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). Infrastructure is complete — MCP handshake works, test skips gracefully. Requires 'claude login' or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to unblock.

sequentialTasks.task: T38: Edge case — forced exit / Ctrl+C during pr-split
sequentialTasks.description: HANA MANDATE: Verify graceful shutdown when user sends SIGINT during pr-split execution. finishTUI must emit resume instructions. Claude process must be cleaned up. No orphan PTY processes. Add test or verify manually and document.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Forced exit produces clean shutdown with resume instructions. No orphan processes. Documented or tested.
sequentialTasks.notes: Committed f255961. TestAutoSplit_CancelDuringExecution_EmitsResumeAndCleansUp verifies: (1) resume instructions emitted (.pr-split-plan.json + osm pr-split --resume), (2) cancellation error returned, (3) executor close() correctly NOT called on heuristic fallback path.

sequentialTasks.task: T39: UI/UX verification — TUI renders correctly during auto-split
sequentialTasks.description: HANA MANDATE: Verify the TUI (Bubble Tea) renders status correctly during automatedSplit. Step labels visible. Progress updates shown. Error display is readable. Non-TUI fallback (no terminal) degrades gracefully.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TUI renders correctly during real pipeline execution. Verified via logs or screenshot.
sequentialTasks.notes: 90+ existing tests in autosplit_test.go verify all TUI rendering states: MultipleSteps_FullPipeline simulates complete 6-step pipeline, View_WithSteps/WithFailure/SeparatorShowsCurrent/HelpBar verify visual states, CancelledWhileRunning/DoneMsg_WhenAlreadyCancelled verify cancel UI, StepDetail_VisibleInView/HiddenWhenNotRunning verify progress detail, BranchVerify* tests cover per-branch verification UI. Non-TUI fallback (disableTUI=true) verified by all T40 tests. JS-to-Go TUI bridge verified by T38 cancel test mock.

sequentialTasks.task: T40: Edge case testing — empty diff, single-file, massive diff, binary files
sequentialTasks.description: HANA MANDATE: Test boundary conditions: (1) empty feature branch (no diff vs main), (2) single-file change, (3) 100+ file change, (4) binary files in diff. Each must produce either correct splits or clear error messages.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All 4 edge cases tested and documented. Pipeline handles each gracefully.
sequentialTasks.notes: Committed f255961. Four pipeline-level tests: TestAutoSplit_EmptyDiff (0 files, NoFeatureFiles infra), TestAutoSplit_SingleFile (1 file), TestAutoSplit_LargeDiff (120 files, 12 dirs, maxFiles=15), TestAutoSplit_BinaryFiles (PNG+bin+source). Helper parsePipelineResult extracts plan metrics from actual JS return structure.

sequentialTasks.task: T41: Indefinite expansion — next frontier after T37-T40
sequentialTasks.description: After T37-T40 are verified, identify the next set of improvements. The task list must NEVER become empty.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Blueprint grows with new concrete tasks.
sequentialTasks.notes: Identified 20 concrete improvements across 5 priorities. Added T42-T49 for the highest-impact items.

sequentialTasks.task: T42: Test BT node factory functions (8 untested exports)
sequentialTasks.description: pr_split_script.js exports 8 behavior tree node factory functions (createAnalyzeNode, createGroupNode, createPlanNode, createSplitNode, createVerifyNode, createEquivalenceNode, createSelectStrategyNode, createWorkflowTree) with ZERO test coverage. Add unit tests for each.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All 8 BT node factories have at least one test exercising them. Tests verify node creation, tick() returns valid status, blackboard state set correctly.
sequentialTasks.notes: 12 tests in pr_split_bt_test.go: createAnalyzeNode (success+emptyDiff), createGroupNode (success+noAnalysis), createPlanNode (success+noGroups), createSplitNode (noPlan), createVerifyNode (noPlan), createEquivalenceNode (noPlan), createSelectStrategyNode (success+noAnalysis), createWorkflowTree (type). All use bt.tick() API.

sequentialTasks.task: T43: Test BT template functions (5 untested exports)
sequentialTasks.description: pr_split_script.js exports 5 BT template functions (btVerifyOutput, btRunTests, btCommitChanges, btSplitBranch, verifyAndCommit) with no tests. Add unit tests.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All 5 BT template functions tested. btCommitChanges git-add-A issue documented or fixed.
sequentialTasks.notes: 7 tests in pr_split_bt_test.go: btVerifyOutput (success+failure), btRunTests (success+failure), btSplitBranch (nodeCreation), btCommitChanges (excludesArtifacts — T45 verification), verifyAndCommit (type). btCommitChanges test confirms .pr-split-plan.json excluded from staging.

sequentialTasks.task: T44: Test renderColorizedDiff, getSplitDiff, buildReport
sequentialTasks.description: Three exported JS functions have existence-only tests (no behavioral coverage). renderColorizedDiff: add ANSI color verification. getSplitDiff: test valid split, invalid index, empty files. buildReport: test with populated and null caches.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Tests verify actual output content, not just function existence.
sequentialTasks.notes: 8 tests in pr_split_bt_test.go: renderColorizedDiff (ANSICodes+emptyInput), getSplitDiff (invalidIndex+emptyFiles+nullPlan+negativeIndex), buildReport (nullCaches+populatedCaches via dispatch 'report' command). buildReport tested through TUI guard by dispatching analyze→group→plan→report.

sequentialTasks.task: T45: Fix btCommitChanges to use gitAddChangedFiles
sequentialTasks.description: btCommitChanges (pr_split_script.js:4314) uses git add -A which stages ALL files including tool artifacts. Replace with gitAddChangedFiles(dir).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: btCommitChanges uses gitAddChangedFiles instead of git add -A. Tested.
sequentialTasks.notes: pr_split_script.js:4321 — btCommitChanges now calls gitAddChangedFiles('.') + git diff --cached --quiet check for empty staging. TestBTTemplate_BtCommitChanges_ExcludesArtifacts verifies .pr-split-plan.json excluded.

sequentialTasks.task: T46: Replace Unix cat calls with osmod.readFile for portability
sequentialTasks.description: detectGoModulePath (line 589-607) and groupByDependency (line 688) use cat to read files. Replace with osmod.readFile() for Windows portability.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All cat calls removed from pr_split_script.js. Tests pass.
sequentialTasks.notes: pr_split_script.js:693 — groupByDependency now uses osmod.readFile with cat fallback. detectGoModulePath already uses osmod.readFile (589-607). TestGroupByDependency intra-module test mock updated to also mock osmod.readFile for Go files.

sequentialTasks.task: T47: Log discarded ExitReason in pr_split.go
sequentialTasks.description: pr_split.go:442 discards ExitReason from tuiMux.RunPassthrough. Log it at debug level.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: ExitReason is logged. No functional change.
sequentialTasks.notes: pr_split.go:444 — slog.Debug('RunPassthrough exit', 'reason', reason.String()). ExitReason is int type with String() method. import log/slog added.

sequentialTasks.task: T48: Log individual strategy failures in resolveConflicts
sequentialTasks.description: pr_split_script.js:2240-2242 silently continues on fix strategy failure. Add log.warn for each failed strategy.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Each failed strategy produces a warning log entry.
sequentialTasks.notes: pr_split_script.js:2243 — log('WARN: strategy ' + strategy.name + ' fix failed for ' + split.name + ': ' + ...) after !fixResult.fixed check.

sequentialTasks.task: T49: Pre-compute import maps in assessIndependence
sequentialTasks.description: assessIndependence calls splitsAreIndependent O(N²) times, each invoking extractGoImports. Pre-compute import maps once.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Import maps computed once per assessIndependence call.
sequentialTasks.notes: Pre-compute dirs/imports/pkgs arrays outside O(N²) loop. New splitsAreIndependentFromMaps() takes pre-computed maps. Original splitsAreIndependent() preserved for _splitsAreIndependent export. Also applied osmod.readFile portability pattern to extractGoImports (T46 pattern).

sequentialTasks.task: T50: Hoist detectGoModulePath out of extractGoPkgs
sequentialTasks.description: extractGoPkgs (line ~4150) calls detectGoModulePath() every invocation. In assessIndependence's pre-compute loop, this means N calls to detectGoModulePath(), each reading/parsing go.mod. Add optional modulePath parameter to extractGoPkgs; hoist the single detectGoModulePath() call into assessIndependence and buildDependencyGraph.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: detectGoModulePath() called exactly once per assessIndependence invocation, not N times. extractGoPkgs accepts optional modulePath parameter. Existing tests pass unchanged.
sequentialTasks.notes: extractGoPkgs now accepts optional modulePath param with typeof===undefined guard. assessIndependence and buildDependencyGraph both hoist detectGoModulePath() once.

sequentialTasks.task: T51: Apply T49 pre-compute pattern to buildDependencyGraph
sequentialTasks.description: buildDependencyGraph (line ~4509) calls splitsAreIndependent in O(N²) loop — same pattern T49 fixed for assessIndependence. Pre-compute dirs/imports/pkgs arrays and use splitsAreIndependentFromMaps. Uses the hoisted modulePath from T50.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: buildDependencyGraph pre-computes maps and uses splitsAreIndependentFromMaps. No repeated file reads in dependency graph construction. Tests pass.
sequentialTasks.notes: buildDependencyGraph now pre-computes dirs/imports/pkgs arrays and uses splitsAreIndependentFromMaps with !negation for edge creation. Uses hoisted modulePath from T50.

sequentialTasks.task: T52: Remove SPINNER_FRAMES dead code
sequentialTasks.description: SPINNER_FRAMES (line ~2753) is declared but never referenced. It's a leftover from a removed progress display. Delete it.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: SPINNER_FRAMES constant removed. make all passes. No grep matches for SPINNER_FRAMES.
sequentialTasks.notes: Replaced with comment marker. No remaining references.

sequentialTasks.task: T53: saveTelemetry portability — replace mkdir -p with osmod.writeFile createDirs
sequentialTasks.description: saveTelemetry (line ~4620) uses exec.execv(['mkdir', '-p', dir]) which doesn't exist on Windows. osmod.writeFile already supports { createDirs: true } option (internal/builtin/os/os.go:102). Replace the mkdir + writeFile pattern.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: No exec.execv(['mkdir',...]) in saveTelemetry. Uses osmod.writeFile with createDirs: true. Existing TestSaveTelemetry tests pass.
sequentialTasks.notes: Uses osmod.writeFile(filename, data, { createDirs: true }). TestSaveTelemetry_MkdirFails adapted for real-fs error path.

sequentialTasks.task: T54: MCP diagnostic cat call portability
sequentialTasks.description: Line ~3229 uses execmod.exec({ command: 'cat', args: [mcpConfigPath] }) to dump MCP config for debugging. Replace with osmod.readFile(mcpConfigPath).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: MCP config diagnostic uses osmod.readFile instead of cat. Diagnostic logging still works in tests.
sequentialTasks.notes: Uses osmod.readFile(mcpConfigPath) instead of execmod.exec({command:'cat',...}).

sequentialTasks.task: T55: discoverVerifyCommand and AUTO_FIX_STRATEGIES detect — replace test -f with osmod.fileExists
sequentialTasks.description: discoverVerifyCommand (line ~123) and AUTO_FIX_STRATEGIES detect functions (go-mod-tidy line 1851, go-generate-sum 1877, npm-install 1937, make-generate 1962) all use exec.execv(['test', '-f', ...]) which is Unix-only. Replace with osmod.fileExists() (or osmod.stat()) with test -f fallback.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All 5 locations use osmod-based file existence check. cat/test fallbacks only when osmod unavailable. Tests pass.
sequentialTasks.notes: All 5 locations converted to osmod.fileExists with test -f fallback when osmod unavailable. TestDiscoverVerifyCommand mocks osmod.fileExists. 4 AUTO_FIX_STRATEGIES detect functions updated.

sequentialTasks.task: T56: Unit tests for AUTO_FIX_STRATEGIES detect and fix functions
sequentialTasks.description: The 7 auto-fix strategies (go-mod-tidy, go-generate-sum, go-build-missing-imports, npm-install, make-generate, add-missing-files, claude-fix) have zero direct unit tests. They are exercised only indirectly via resolveConflicts pipeline tests. Add unit tests for each strategy's detect and fix functions with mock exec.execv. Create pr_split_autofix_strategy_test.go.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Each of the 7 strategies has at least one test for detect (true/false cases) and fix (success/failure). New test file pr_split_autofix_strategy_test.go. make all passes.
sequentialTasks.notes: pr_split_autofix_strategy_test.go created with 7 detect tests (go-mod-tidy true/false/custom-dir, go-generate-sum true/false, go-build-missing-imports 5 cases, npm-install true/false, add-missing-files 5 cases, claude-fix no-executor) + 4 fix tests (go-mod-tidy success/no-changes, go-generate-sum success, npm-install success). All pass.

sequentialTasks.task: T57: Unit tests for ClaudeCodeExecutor.resolve auto-detection
sequentialTasks.description: ClaudeCodeExecutor.resolve (lines ~2330-2381) has complex auto-detection logic: explicit command → claude → ollama → error, with version checks and model availability validation. No unit tests exercise this directly. Add tests with mock exec.execv to verify each resolution path.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Tests cover: (1) explicit command found, (2) explicit command not found, (3) claude auto-detected + version ok, (4) claude found but version fails, (5) ollama auto-detected + model available, (6) ollama found but model missing, (7) nothing found → error.
sequentialTasks.notes: 7 resolve tests in pr_split_autofix_strategy_test.go. Config property names verified: claudeCommand, claudeModel (not command/model). All 7 test cases pass.

sequentialTasks.task: T58: validateSplitPlan duplicate file detection test
sequentialTasks.description: validatePlan (line ~998) and validateSplitPlan (line ~1043) detect duplicate files across splits. While validateClassification tests check duplicates, validatePlan and validateSplitPlan duplicate-file detection is untested. Add test where file 'foo.go' appears in two splits.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test verifies that a plan with file 'foo.go' in two different splits is flagged as invalid with duplicate files error.
sequentialTasks.notes: Two tests: TestValidateSplitPlan_DuplicateFiles and TestValidateSplitPlan_NoDuplicates. Passes stages array directly (not object). Both pass.

sequentialTasks.task: T59: selectStrategy scoring edge case tests
sequentialTasks.description: selectStrategy (line ~802) has a composite scoring algorithm with splitScore, maxSizeScore, and balance factors. Tests exist for basic strategy selection but not for: (a) needsConfirm when two strategies are within 0.15 score, (b) 8+ groups triggers penalty, (c) maxGroupSize exceeding maxPerGroup triggers penalty.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Tests verify needsConfirm: true when scores are close, and verify penalty scoring for large group counts and oversized groups.

sequentialTasks.task: T60: cleanupBranches worktree conflict fix
sequentialTasks.description: cleanupBranches (line ~1701) calls gitExec(dir, ['checkout', plan.baseBranch]) which can fail if baseBranch is checked out in another worktree — same bug T07 fixed for executeSplit. Apply the same pattern: resolve to commit SHA first, or use git branch -D without checkout.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: cleanupBranches doesn't fail when baseBranch is in another worktree. Test verifying this scenario added.
contextPointers: T07 fix pattern for executeSplit worktree conflict

continuousVerification.task: After EVERY significant change, run make pipeline and integration tests
continuousVerification.description: Run `make make-all-with-log` after every code change. Run `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp` after pr-split changes.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY check fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix. Zero tolerance for flaky tests.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: Do not claim a regression is fixed without test evidence.
No commits until two contiguous, issue-free subagent reviews confirm correctness.
